chief_complaint: A 34-year-old woman presented with progressive lower limb weakness and numbness.
history: The patient's condition worsened despite prolonged anti-tuberculous treatment. She had no significant past medical history, and her medications included anti-tuberculous therapy.
exam: Vital signs were not provided. Physical examination revealed progressive lower limb weakness and numbness.
labs: Cerebrospinal fluid analysis revealed elevated protein (not quantified), decreased glucose (not quantified), and chloride levels (not quantified). MRI showed spinal meningeal enhancement, an intradural extramedullary mass compressing the spinal cord.
correct_diagnosis: Spinal malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumor (MPNST)
differentials: Spinal malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumor (MPNST)
correct_ranking: Spinal malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumor (MPNST)
